page: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on an arm64 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core and no hyperthreading. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is a key feature, consisting of 11250 GB of NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 28 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 404.61 GB/sec and write speeds at 201.28 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier bzip3 decompression speed of 511.7 GB/sec and a strong multi-core Geekbench score of 24079.0. Database operations perform exceptionally well, scoring in the top 10% on Passmark database tests and achieving 98.01 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Static web serving throughput is also in the top tier at 102.83 GB/sec. However, single-core CPU performance is average, and LLM text generation with small models is relatively weak at 55.51 tokens per second.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the i8g.12xlarge is driven by its high storage density and the ARM-based Graviton architecture, which offers a favorable performance-per-watt profile compared to traditional x86 architectures. The integration of 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing resource density for data-heavy workloads. The primary tradeoff lies in its single-core performance and the lack of x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, which limits its suitability for workloads dependent on those vector extensions.

Given its high local storage capacity, top-tier database operation benchmarks, and strong memory bandwidth, the i8g.12xlarge is highly suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL stores, and caching layers like Redis. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and static web serving. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring GPU acceleration, as it contains no GPUs.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on an arm64 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 384.0 GB of RAM and 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations, alongside a 28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core performance is average and it lacks x86 vector extensions, its high storage density and ARM-based architecture provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. The i8g.12xlarge is highly suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is a storage-optimized arm64 server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized server combining high-capacity local NVMe SSDs with strong memory bandwidth and multi-core processing efficiency.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores running at 2.8 GHz.
11250 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth.
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression.
28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data processing.
